name: Ascendia Bank
description_short: Ascendia Bank is a Northern New Jersey community bank formerly known as Glen Rock Savings Bank, offering personal and business banking, mortgages, and deposit products.
description_long:
Ascendia Bank traces its roots to 1922 when The Building and Loan Association of Ridgewood was established to provide savings accounts and residential mortgages to local New Jersey residents. Originally located in Glen Rock, the bank expanded to multiple locations and rebranded as Ascendia Bank following a 2015 merger with Llewellyn-Edison Savings Bank. The bank now operates four branches across Northern New Jersey with over $260 million in assets and maintains its focus on community banking.

Ascendia Bank offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business banking products. For personal banking, they provide checking and savings accounts (including Kasasa Cash and Kasasa Saver accounts), money market accounts, and certificates of deposit (15-month CDs at 3.75% APY as of March 2026). Mortgage services include 15, 20, and 30-year fixed-rate mortgages with rates advertised at 6.125% (30-year fixed). Business customers can access commercial lending solutions, business checking accounts with tiered APY rates up to 2.25%, and money market accounts. Digital services include online banking, mobile banking for account opening and check deposits, online bill pay, and mortgage application capabilities.

Ascendia Bank distinguishes itself through its deep local roots and community focus, emphasizing personalized service and market expertise in its commercial lending. The bank highlights its stability through decades of consistent leadership (only two CEOs from 1960-2016) and active community engagement, including sponsorships of local chamber events. Its digital capabilities rival larger institutions while maintaining a local banking approach, and it offers tiered deposit products that reward larger balances.

The bank's primary limitation is geographic scope—it operates only in Northern New Jersey, making it inaccessible to customers outside this region. As a smaller community bank, product offerings are more limited compared to national institutions. Mortgage rates published on the website (6.125%) appear higher than national averages for the period, though rates may vary by application. The bank caters primarily to established community members rather than serving as a specialist in any particular financial niche.
services: Money market accounts with tiered APY rates
Mobile banking with check deposit and account opening
pros: Established local bank with 100+ year history (founded 1922) providing community stability and reliability
Competitive CD rates at 3.75% APY on 15-month certificates with low $500 minimum deposit
Tiered money market and business checking accounts offering 2.50% and 2.25% APY respectively on balances over $50,000
Full mobile banking suite allowing account opening, check mobile deposits, bill pay, and mortgage applications from anywhere
Personalized commercial lending with flexible terms, fast approvals, and local market expertise
Active community engagement and local business partnerships supporting Northern NJ communities
Multiple Northern NJ branch locations with online and in-person service options
cons: Geographic limitation—operates only in Northern New Jersey, excluding most of the country
Advertised 30-year mortgage rate of 6.125% appears higher than national average rates for the period
Limited product specialization compared to national banks—no distinct niche or unique offerings beyond standard community banking
Smaller asset base ($260 million) may limit lending capacity or product innovation versus larger institutions
Website content does not specify checking account APY rates, monthly fees, or minimum balance requirements for basic accounts
best_for: Northern New Jersey residents seeking local personalized banking with community focus and branch accessibility
Small to mid-sized business owners in North Jersey needing flexible commercial lending and customized loan solutions
Conservative savers looking for stable, established banking with competitive rates on CDs and high-balance money market accounts
Homebuyers in Northern New Jersey seeking mortgage services with local market expertise and personalized loan processing
diagnosis: Ascendia Bank is best for Northern New Jersey residents and small business owners seeking stable, personalized community banking with competitive deposit rates and local lending expertise. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation—the bank operates exclusively in Northern New Jersey, making it inaccessible to customers outside this region. Potential customers should compare mortgage and CD rates with national banks, as advertised rates may not be the most competitive nationally.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
